title: Angelica oreoselinum
taxonomy: Angelica oreoselinum is a plant species in the family Apiaceae. It is native to Europe and Asia, and belongs to the genus Angelica. The plant is further classified under the order Apiales and the kingdom Plantae. This classification places Angelica oreoselinum within the larger context of flowering plants. The species is also known as lesser angelica or field angelica.
uses: Angelica oreoselinum has been used in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties. It has been traditionally employed to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant is also believed to have laxative and carminative effects, making it useful for digestive issues. Furthermore, Angelica oreoselinum is thought to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against infections and oxidative stress. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of this plant.
benefits: Angelica oreoselinum, also known as European angelica or wild celery, is believed to have several health benefits. Its roots and leaves are rich in vitamins and minerals, making it a potential remedy for various ailments. The plant has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, Angelica oreoselinum is thought to possess antioxidant properties, which could protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Some studies suggest that the plant may also have a role in supporting the immune system and reducing the risk of certain diseases.
properties: Angelica oreoselinum has been traditionally used for its various therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is also believed to possess antioxidant and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe and calm the nervous system. Its roots, leaves, and seeds are used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, from digestive problems to skin conditions. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to reduce pain and swelling, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as arthritis. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Angelica oreoselinum.
constituents: Angelica oreoselinum contains a variety of active constituents, including volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The plant's volatile oils have been reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Flavonoids found in Angelica oreoselinum may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The phenolic acids present in the plant have been shown to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. These active constituents contribute to the plant's potential medicinal uses.
parts: Angelica oreoselinum is a plant species, but specific information on its medicinal parts could not be verified.
preparations: Angelica oreoselinum is used in traditional herbal preparations, often employed for their aromatic and medicinal properties. In folk medicine, the plant has been used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. The roots and leaves of the plant are commonly used in herbal remedies, which are typically prepared through infusions or decoctions. Herbal preparations made from Angelica oreoselinum are also valued for their potential antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. These preparations are often used externally to soothe skin irritations and internally to support overall well-being.
side_effects: Angelica oreoselinum, also known as European angelica, is used in traditional medicine but can cause adverse reactions in some individuals. The side effects of Angelica oreoselinum can include allergic reactions, such as skin irritation and respiratory issues. Ingesting large quantities of Angelica oreoselinum may also lead to stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea. Additionally, it may interact with certain medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications. Pregnant women and breastfeeding mothers should exercise caution when using Angelica oreoselinum due to its potential effects on the fetus or baby.
intro: Angelica oreoselinum, also known as wild angelica, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in relieving digestive issues, reducing pain, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Angelica oreoselinum include essential oils, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, such as sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and polyacetylenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in traditional remedies for its carminative and antispasmodic effects.
